*   Natural Gas Methane Content: approximately 96%

title: 2012GRC948
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 8]
Here's the breakdown of the technical content extracted from the provided text and images:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   Equation 1: Xavg = integral(0 to R) [X(r) * 2(pi) * rdr] / [pi * (R squared)]
*   Equation 2: XNorm = X(r) / Xavg

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**3. Tables:**

*   NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**
*  Figure 2: Combustor assembly in the Single Nozzle Rig:
    *   Components: Nozzle, Combustion liner, Eccentric reducer, Rig mounted on mid frame

*  Figure 3: Combustor Air flow splits:
    *   Components: Main combustion air, Cap cooling air, Liner cooling air

*  Figure 4: Combustion Liner:
    * Components: Primary Zone or P Zone, Liner Zone, Premixed natural gas injection, Burner Tube

*  Figure 5: Nozzle illustration:
    * Components: Swirler, Nozzle Center Body

* Figure 6: 1D traverse capability illustration.
    * 1D Traverse

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   O2 level: 15%
*   P3 (load 1): 140 psia
*   P3 (load 2): 225 psia
*   T3 (load 1): 715 F
*   T3 (load 2): 865 F
*   Combustor residence time: 18 ms
*   ΔP/P3: 2.5 to 4 %
*   Data acquisition frequency: 1 Hz
*   Number of data points averaged: 8
*   NOx standard deviation: ±1 ppm
*   P3 standard deviation: ±1.2 psi (0.08 bar)
*   T3 standard deviation: ±4°F (2.2 K)

    *   X-axis: TCombustor Exit [°F]
*   Curves:
    *   MaxPZoneTemp_Distil (stars)
    *   MaxLinerTemp_Distil (dashed blue)
    *   MaxPZoneTemp_NatGas (squares)

*   Key values:
    * Flame temperature ~2650F, temperature difference of ~100F between distil and natural gas.

**3. Tables:**

**4. Diagrams (Figure 7):**

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Flame temperature agreement (metered air fuel/O2 emissions): within 1%
*   P3 ~ 139 Psia, T3 ~ 713 F (Pressure and Temperature parameter)
*   Camera view angle: 90 degrees
*   Quartz window dimensions: 5.5" x 3"
*   Primary zone temperature averaged over 8 thermocouples.
*   Standard Deviation readings from 8 thermocouples.
*   Fuel comparison made at part load operating conditions.

title: 2012GRC948
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 10]
Here's the technical content extracted from the images, broken down by category:

**1. Mathematical equations**

*   P3 ~ 139 Psia
*   T3 ~ 713F

**2. Graphs/charts:**

*   **Figure 9: Average metal skin temperature in the primary zone vs. combustor exit temperature for non-premixed natural gas and dry Dist II at part load conditions.**

    *   X-axis: TCombustor Exit [°F], values range from 1950 to 2850
    *   Y-axis: Temperature [°F], values range from 700 to 1400
    *   Curves: Tavg_P_Dill_Load 1, Tavg_P_NatGas_Load 1

*   **Figure 13: Average metal skin temperature in the liner zone vs. combustor exit temperature for non-premixed natural gas and dry Dist II at part load conditions.**

    *   X-axis: TCombustor Exit [°F], values range from 1950 to 2950
    *   Y-axis: Temperature [°F], values range from 700 to 1900,
    *   Curves: Tavg_Liner_Dill_Load 1, Tavg_Liner_NatGas_Load 1

**3. Tables:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

*   **Figure 10: Non-Premixed Natural as Flame Shape**

    *   Components: Burner Tube, Corner Recirculation Zone, Flow direction, MERA07

*   **Figure 11: DistII (dry) flame shape**

    *   Components: Burner Tube, Corner Recirculation Zone, Flow direction, CAMERA07

*   **Figure 12: Premixed flame shape**

    *   Components: Burner Tube, Corner Recirculation Zone, Flow direction

**5. Key numbers, constants, parameters:**

*   NOx emissions are reduced by ~ 95% when compared to the non-premixed natural gas mode of operation.
*   Carbon Monoxide (CO) emissions from natural gas is roughly 85 ppm lower over the temperature range of interest. NOx emissions are 35-80% lower for natural gas over the range of interest with more reduction at higher flame temperatures.

title: 2012GRC948
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 11]
Here's the technical information extracted from the gas turbine research page:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   Eq. (2)  [Implied: This is used to normalize O2 emissions, with the y-axis representing a percentage from the average.]

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Figure 14: Average NOx15 and CO15 vs. combustor exit temperature for non-premixed natural gas and dry Dist II at part load conditions.**

    *   Axes:
        *   Y-axis: NOx15 & CO15 (ppmvd)
        *   X-axis: TCombustorExit ["F]
    *   Curves:
        *   NOx15_50%load_DII: decreasing from around 800 ppmvd to around 100ppmvd
        *   CO1S_DII: increasing from 0 to around 300 ppmvd
        *   NOx15_50%load_NatGas_Nonpremix: decreasing from 700 ppmvd to around 100ppmvd
        *   CO15_NatGas: increasing slightly from 0 to around 100ppmvd
    *   Key Values: P3~139 Psia, T3~713F

*   **Figure 15: Normalized O2 emissions profiles over the combustor exit for non-premixed natural gas operation.**

    *   Axes:
        *   Y-axis: O2 (Norm%)
        *   X-axis: Radius (Inch)
    *   Curves:
        *   O2Norm_TCombustorExit = 2000F
        *   O2Norm_TCombustorExit = 2502F
        *   O2Norm_TCombustorExit = 2705F
    *   Key Values: P3=139 Psia, T3=713F

*   **Figure 16: Temperature profile over the combustor exit for non-premixed natural gas operation at an average exit temperature of 2700F.**

    *   Axes:
        *   Y-axis: Hot gases Temperature (F)
        *   X-axis: Radius (Inch)
    *   Curves:
    *   TCombustorExit=2705F
    *   Key Values: P3=139 Psia, T3=713F; average exit temperature of 2700F

**3. Tables:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Variability of O2 emissions: ~40% from the walls to the center point.
*   Combustor exit temperature exceeds 2700F, variability increases to 70%.
*   Variability of NOx emissions: around 170% for both non-premixed natural gas and dry liquid fuel.
*   Average exit temperature: 2700F
*   Temperature at the center stream: close to 3200F.
*   Non-premixed natural gas amplitude of oscillation: 1-2 psi pk-pk
*   Dry liquid fuel amplitude of oscillation: 5-7 psi pk-pk
*   Peak frequency for dry liquid fuel operation: ~220 Hz
*   Peak frequency for natural gas operation: 150-220 Hz
*  P3~139 Psia, T3~713F

title: 2012GRC948
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 14]
Here's the extracted technical content from the provided text and images:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   NOx is proportional to P^n

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Figure 27:**
    *   Axes:
        *   X-axis: Tcombustor Exit [°F]
        *   Left Y-axis: P' [psi pk-pk]
        *   Right Y-axis: Frequency (Hz)
    *   Curves:
        *   Acoustics (P") psi pk-pk_Wet Liquid Fuel
        *   Acoustics (P") psi pk-pk_Dry Liquid Fuel
        *   Frequency (Hz)_Wet Liquid Fuel
        *   Frequency (Hz)_Distil
    *   Key values:
        *   Wet vs. Dry Liquid Fuel, Load 1, P3=139 Psia, T3=713F

*   **Figure 28:**
    *   Axes:
        *   X-axis: Tcombustor Exit [°F]
        *   Y-axis: Temperature [°F]
    *   Curves:
        *   MaxPZoneTemp_Distil_50%
        *   MaxLinerTemp_Distll_50%
        *   MaxPZoneTemp_Distil_100%
        *   MaxLinerTemp_Distll_100%
        *   Distll, Dry, 50% (P3~139 Psia, T3~713F) Vs. 100% (P3~220Psia, T3=838F)

*   **Figure 29:**
    *   Axes:
        *   X-axis: TCombustor Exit [°F]
        *   Y-axis: Temperature [°F]
    *   Curves:
        *   Tavg_Liner_DII_50%
        *   Tavg_Liner_Distll_100%
        *   Distll, Dry, 50% (P3~139 Psia, T3~713F) Vs. 100% (P3~220Psia, T3=838F)

**3. Tables:**

*   NO_TABLE_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

*   NO_DIAGRAM_CONTENT

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   P3=139 Psia, T3=713F (Wet vs. Dry Liquid Fuel, Load 1)
*   P3 ~ 139 Psia, T3 ~ 713F (50% operating conditions)
*   P3 = 220 Psia, T3 = 838F (100% operating conditions)
*   The exponent varies from 0.9-1.05
*   0.5 pressure exponent (or n) where NOx is proportional to  P^n
*   Primary zone maximum metal temperature 280 F to 360 F higher at full load operating conditions.
*   Liner Zone maximum metal temperature increases by 80F to 150F.
*   The metal temperature in the liner zone is ~130F higher at high power conditions.
*   T3 increased by ~ 130F from part load to higher load operating conditions.

title: 2012GRC948
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 15]
Here's the technical content extracted from the provided image:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   None evident

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Figure 31: pressure exponent n as function of flame temperature.**
    *   Axes:
        *   x-axis: TCombustor Exit [°F] (2100 to 3000)
        *   y-axis: n (pressure exponent) (0.5 to 1.1)
    *   Curves: single curve showing increase in pressure exponent with temperature.

*   **Figure 32: peak frequencies and corresponding amplitudes for low power and high power operating conditions vs. combustor exit temperature.**
    *   Axes (left):
        *   y-axis: P' [psi pk-pk] (0.0 to 8.0)
    *   Axes (right):
        *   y-axis: Frequency (Hz) (0 to 300)
        *   x-axis: TCombustor Exit [°F] (2100 to 2900)
    *   Curves:
        *   Acoustics (P) psi pk-pk Full Load Dry
        *   Acoustics (P) psi pk-pk Part Load Dry
        *   Frequency(Hz) Full Load Dry
        *   Frequency(Hz) Part load Dry

*   **Figure 30: Average NOx15 and CO15 vs. combustor exit temperature for dry liquid fuel operation at low power and high power operating conditions**
    *   Axes:
        *   y-axis: N0x15 & CO15 (ppmvd) (0 to 600)
        *   x-axis: TCombustor Exit [°F] (2150 to 2950)
    *   Curves:
        *   Nox15_Dil_50%
        *   CO15_Dil_50%
        *   NOx15_Distll_100%
        *   CO15_Distil_100%

**3. Tables:**

*   None visible

**4. Diagrams:**

*   None visible

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   CO reduction:   84-66%
*   P3 = 139 Psia, T3=713F
*   Dynamics is provoked by oscillations at ~ 200Hz frequency
*   50% (P3~139 Psia, T3~713F) Vs. 100% (P3~220Psia, T3=838F)
*   Increase in head end running: 280-360F
*   Natural gas to dry liquid fuel resulted in 100F increase in average head end temperature.
*   Tail section was 50F lower.
*   liquid fuel with water : resulted in 300F reduction in the head end temperature.
*   Tail section was running 100F higher.
